tool_equivalents.dbt: Passes type check but breaks value semantics
tool_equivalents.pact: Consumer pact fails if type was declared

operator_tooling.meaning: Lower numeric bound changed; symmetric risk to range_maximum_change.
operator_tooling.kafka_avro_json: Same as tightening `minimum` in schema-aware pipelines—old producers may still send smaller values.
operator_tooling.dbt: Adjust minimum checks and fix rows below the new floor before promoting.

add_required_field.meaning: Every record must now carry this field; absent values break consumers written against the old contract.
add_required_field.kafka_avro_json: Adding a required property without a default is backward-incompatible for old consumers; use optional-then-required rollout or a new subject/version.
add_required_field.dbt: Add nullable column → backfill → then enforce `not_null` / contract test; fail CI if seeds omit the column.
add_required_field.schema_registry: Same compatibility class as Confluent BACKWARD blocked: existing readers cannot assume the field exists.

add_nullable_field.meaning: New optional field; old producers/consumers may omit it.
add_nullable_field.kafka_avro_json: Purely additive field (optional) is usually backward compatible; bump schema version per your registry policy.
add_nullable_field.dbt: `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN` nullable; update `sources.yml` / contract YAML; rarely blocks `dbt compile`.
add_nullable_field.schema_registry: Typically allowed under BACKWARD; still notify Flink/SQL consumers that a new attribute may appear.

remove_field.meaning: Column/attribute removed from the contract; anything still selecting it fails.
remove_field.kafka_avro_json: Removing a field breaks readers that decode it; use deprecation window, dual schema, or new topic major version.
remove_field.dbt: Downstream `ref()` models fail if they still `select` the column; drop in staging only after lineage is clean.
remove_field.schema_registry: BLOCKED under BACKWARD/FULL until no consumer depends on the field.

type_change.meaning: Wire representation changed (e.g. string → number); reinterpretation and Serde errors are likely.
type_change.kafka_avro_json: Incompatible type swap for the same subject is rejected; may require new topic or compatibility override (discouraged).
type_change.dbt: Explicit `cast` in staging may fail on legacy strings; add cleansing migration + data-quality test before tightening contract.
type_change.schema_registry: Equivalent to incompatible evolution—do not auto-register without human review.

range_maximum_change.meaning: Upper numeric bound tightened; values that were valid may now violate the contract.
range_maximum_change.kafka_avro_json: If bounds are encoded in schema (Avro/JSON Schema `maximum`), tightening can fail registration or reject in-flight records.
range_maximum_change.dbt: Tighten `accepted_range` / custom test; quarantine or clamp rows above the new max.
range_maximum_change.schema_registry: Treat like narrowing: backward readers may still emit now-illegal values.

range_minimum_change.meaning: Lower numeric bound changed; symmetric risk to range_maximum_change.
range_minimum_change.kafka_avro_json: Same as tightening `minimum` in schema-aware pipelines—old producers may still send smaller values.
range_minimum_change.dbt: Adjust minimum checks and fix rows below the new floor before promoting.

enum_value_removed.meaning: A previously legal enum literal disappeared; pipelines still emitting it will fail validation.
enum_value_removed.kafka_avro_json: Removing enum symbols is backward incompatible; drain producers or dual-write before registration.
enum_value_removed.dbt: Update `accepted_values` and dimension tables; legacy fact rows may fail until backfilled/mapped.
enum_value_removed.schema_registry: BLOCKED for backward compatibility until consumers stop using the removed symbol.

enum_value_added.meaning: New allowed categorical value; old code paths usually still work.
enum_value_added.kafka_avro_json: Adding enum values is often backward compatible; Flink SQL or dbt seeds that enumerate all literals need updating.
enum_value_added.dbt: Extend `accepted_values` / seed lists; add test row for the new category.
enum_value_added.schema_registry: Generally permitted; still broadcast to analytics owners.

no_material_change.meaning: No clause the analyzer treats as material (ordering/comments drift, etc.).
no_material_change.kafka_avro_json: No Kafka subject action implied by this row alone.
no_material_change.dbt: No migration triggered from this classification.
no_material_change.schema_registry: No registration delta required from this row.

classification_error.meaning: Classifier raised; treat as breaking until an engineer confirms.
classification_error.kafka_avro_json: Block automated schema promotion to prod topics.
classification_error.dbt: Hold merge; reproduce diff with explicit `--snapshot-old` / `--snapshot-new`.
classification_error.schema_registry: Manual review only—do not rely on compatibility verdict from this row.

migration_checklist: [REQUIRED] Write migration script in outputs/migrate/ before deploying
[REQUIRED] Re-run ValidationRunner after migration to confirm clean
rollback_plan: Rollback to snapshot '20260404T192053Z.yaml' under contract 'week3-document-refinery-extractions'; restore prior artifacts before retrying promotion.

steps: Check out contract schema from snapshot file schema_snapshots/week3-document-refinery-extractions/20260404T192053Z.yaml.
Revert generated_contracts YAML and dbt models to the commit that produced that snapshot (or regenerate from frozen inputs).
Notify all registry subscribers for this contract_id; pause ENFORCE gates until rollback is verified.
Run: python contracts/runner.py --contract generated_contracts/<matching>.yaml --data <stable jsonl> --mode AUDIT.
Open a new migration PR before re-attempting the breaking schema promotion.
